title: Yi Jou (Ruby) Liao
text:
Hi friends!
I’m currently a PhD Candidate in the Wyatt Lab at the Vancouver Prostate Centre studying prostate cancer genomics, as part of the University of British Columbia (UBC) Genome Science and Technology (GSAT) program.
Previously, I graduated from University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) in 2022 with a BS in Computational & Systems Biology with a Concentration in Data Science. I was a Research Assistant at the Graeber Lab, where I contributed to a project characterizing recurrent ovarian cancers and assisted with the UCLA Metabolomics Center.
Check out my CV or LinkedIn for more of my background.
In my free time, I enjoy listening to music (indie/alt/pop/k-pop/electronic) and playing video games (simulation/sandbox/story-driven).

title: Workflow setup
text: General overview of my digital workflow setup. Ocassionally updated.

title: Workflow setup
text:
Running R in VSCode
R in Visual Studio Code - Set R path setting to R executable path (ex. /usr/local/bin/R)

R extension
radian: alternative R console with syntax highlighting and multiline editing

Set bracketed paste to true
Set R terminal to radian path (ex. /home/yliao/miniforge3/bin/radian)
If CTRL+Enter keeps creating a new R Interactive session, can try turning “Always Use Active Terminal” off

R Debugger

vscode-R: Interacting with R terminals
For self-managed R terminals, add these lines to ~/.Rprofile:

if (interactive() && Sys.getenv("RSTUDIO") == "") {
  Sys.setenv(TERM_PROGRAM = "vscode")
  source(file.path(Sys.getenv(
    if (.Platform$OS.type == "windows") "USERPROFILE" else "HOME"
  ), ".vscode-R", "init.R"))
}

Snippet of R VSCode settings (JSON)

  "r.plot.useHttpgd": true,
  "r.lsp.diagnostics": false,
  "r.alwaysUseActiveTerminal": true,
  "r.rterm.windows": "C:\\Users\\yliao\\AppData\\Local\\Programs\\Python\\Python312\\Scripts\\radian.exe",
  "r.rpath.windows": "C:\\Program Files\\R\\R-4.3.2\\bin\\R.exe",
  "r.rpath.linux": "/usr/local/bin/R",
  "r.rterm.linux": "/home/yliao/miniforge3/bin/radian",
  "r.removeLeadingComments": true,
  "r.session.levelOfObjectDetail": "Normal",
  "r.bracketedPaste": true,

\(\LaTeX\) in VSCode

LaTeX Workshop

title: Workflow setup
text:
Community plugins
Core:

Dataview: enable data index and query for MD files
Git: back up entire vault to a GitHub repository

I only run Git sync on my laptop to prevent weird conflict issues across multiple devices - there is a “Disable on this device” setting.

Templater: template plugin that can execute JavaScript code, can automate creation of daily notes

Dann Berg: My Obsidian Daily Note Template


Zotero:

Zotero Integration: add cite-key & citations from Zotero, import notes & metadata as templated LitNotes

Danny Hatcher: Zotero Obsidian Integration LitNotes overview
Alexandra Phelan: An Updated Academic Workflow: Zotero & Obsidian

Pandoc Reference List: display a formatted reference in Obsidian’s sidebar for each pandoc citekey in the active document

Functionality:

Advanced Tables: improved table manipulation
Extended MathJax: extends MathJax LaTeX support
File Cleaner Redux: helper to remove unused/empty MD files and attachments
Better Word Count: counts words of selected text in editor

Import/Export:

Excel to Markdown Table: paste tables from Excel/Sheets as MD tables
Markdown Export: export directory/single MD (including pictures) to a folder

title: Workflow setup
text:
Templates

Daily Template
LitNote

LitNote: Clinical Trial
LitNote: Reviews


The Daily notes Core plugin automatically places new file locations in my /Daily folder under custom format YYYY/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD-ddd.
For LitNotes, I use Templater to automatically apply these templates depending on the folder that the note is created in.
